schema_note: Committed signal registry: every cross-repo signal this repo bakes or mirrors into a */data/*.json file and re-renders on sibling surfaces. Each row is name -> baker -> raw-URL -> consumers, so a new drift/parity fan-out is a data edit here, not a code hunt across services. Rows are REAL and verified against the tree by tests/test_signal_registry.py: `baker` and `committed_mirror` are repo-relative paths that must exist (or null when the signal is baked in another repo / maintained by hand), every `consumers` entry is a repo-relative file that must exist AND reference the signal by its `raw_url` basename, and `raw_url` is the raw.githubusercontent.com URL consumers fetch. Generalises the arcade<->web_presence join in tests/test_registry_drift.py. Add a row (or a consumer) by PR; the test keeps every declared reference honest.

row_schema.name: str, unique, kebab-case signal id
row_schema.description: str, one line: what the signal carries
row_schema.producer_repo: str, owner/repo where the signal is baked/committed
row_schema.baker: str repo-relative path to the generator, or null (PR-maintained registry or cross-repo baker)
row_schema.committed_mirror: str repo-relative path to the committed JSON in THIS repo, or null when the mirror lives in producer_repo
row_schema.raw_url: str, the raw.githubusercontent.com URL consumers fetch (env-overridable consumers default to this)
row_schema.consumers: list of {path, note}: repo-relative files in THIS repo that re-render/read the signal

signals.name: release-drift
signals.description: Per-repo release-tag drift flags baked by review; re-rendered read-only on the dashboard /status card and the console board attn chip without recomputing drift.

consumers.note: native producer surface — renders releases.json site-wide in the review service
control-plane board parity — fetches the mirror over github._get(raw=True), re-renders the drift flag
dashboard /status parity — RELEASES_JSON_URL fetch + release_drift() shaper

signals.name: fleet-arcade
signals.description: The Fleet Arcade game registry (slug, availability, url); the reachability source of truth joined against web-presence by the registry-drift guard.

consumers.note: native loader — reads arcade.json from disk, derives is_live/has_link/link_url for the /arcade surface
dashboard arcade-counts parity — ARCADE_JSON_URL fetch of the same registry

signals.name: web-presence-directory
signals.description: Every web surface the estate owns (id, status, url); read at request time by the control-plane /directory and joined against fleet-arcade by the registry-drift guard.

consumers.note: native loader — load_registry() reads web_presence.json at request time, probes each row's liveness
control-plane /directory route — renders the loaded web_presence registry

signals.name: botsite-site-feed
signals.description: The bot's public marketing/reference content baked in superbot; re-rendered as the botsite marketing site.

consumers.note: SITE_JSON_URL fetch over raw.githubusercontent.com (no token); the botsite content source

signals.name: dashboard-inventory-feed
signals.description: The bot's own inventory (functions/commands/settings/access/env/ideas/bugs) baked in superbot; re-rendered read-only on the developer dashboard.

consumers.note: DASHBOARD_JSON_URL fetch — the dashboard inventory source

signals.name: console-board-feed
signals.description: The bot's public console board snapshot baked in superbot; re-rendered on the dashboard.

consumers.note: CONSOLE_JSON_URL fetch — the console board source on the dashboard
